text.title: Continuous Delivery Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Is a commit on main not releasable as-is?

conclusion.verdict: stop and fix; CD requires unconditional releasability

branch.question: Is there a non-emergency manual step in the deploy pipeline?

conclusion.verdict: automate it before merging more changes

branch.question: Is a single deploy dropping or renaming schema?

conclusion.verdict: split into expand → code → contract phases

branch.question: Is a new flag missing owner or expiry?

conclusion.verdict: reject in CI; add both fields

branch.question: Does the deploy job lack a rollback step?
Does the regulatory regime mandate per-release human approval?

conclusion.verdict: use Continuous Integration + manual release; full CD is out of scope
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
